 {"id":"frankenredis-7ews","title":"Wire LATENCY subcommands to real LatencyTracker data","description":"# Wire LATENCY subcommands to real LatencyTracker data

## What
Replace the stub latency_cmd in fr-command/src/lib.rs (line ~11387) with real implementations that read from Store's LatencyTracker.

## Changes needed
- latency_cmd must accept &Store parameter (change dispatch_argv call site)
- LATENCY LATEST: return store.latency_tracker.latency_latest() formatted as [[event, timestamp, latest_ms, max_ms], ...]
- LATENCY HISTORY event: return store.latency_tracker.latency_history(event) as [[timestamp, duration_ms], ...]
- LATENCY RESET [event ...]: call store.latency_tracker.latency_reset() and return count of reset events
- LATENCY GRAPH event: generate ASCII art from history samples (implementation detail: use Redis's exact format with ± indicators and time axis)
- LATENCY DOCTOR: analyze samples and generate diagnostic text (e.g., "I have no latency reports to analyze" when empty, or "Command latency spikes detected..." when samples exist)

## LATENCY GRAPH format (from Redis)
```
event - min: Xms, max: Yms
+---+
|   |
|  #|
|###|
+---+
HH:MM
```
Each column is one sample. Height proportional to duration. Time labels at bottom.

## LATENCY DOCTOR format
Redis produces multi-paragraph analysis. For initial implementation:
- If no samples: "I have no latency reports to analyze. Be happy!"
- If samples exist: "I have reports for the following events:\n- {event}: {count} samples, max {max}ms\n..."

## Depends on
- frankenredis-a4sf (LatencyTracker infrastructure)

## Testing
- Conformance: update core_server.json LATENCY cases to expect real data after CONFIG SET latency-monitor-threshold + command execution
- Unit test: record_latency_sample then verify latency_latest output format

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (latency_cmd, dispatch_argv call site)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:18:41.386191914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:16:48.609424066Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:16:48.609164495Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-7ews","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-a4sf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:21:34.117001780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-7o5","title":"[EPIC] Stream and Persistence Completeness","description":"## Overview

Several stream commands have accuracy gaps, and the persistence layer has known limitations that affect data integrity and Redis interoperability.

## Stream Issues

1. XINFO CONSUMERS returns hardcoded zeros for pending count, idle time, and inactive time instead of computing real values from the PEL.

2. XPENDING extended form doesn't support the IDLE option (Redis 6.2+). Commands like \"XPENDING key group IDLE 5000 - + 10\" return WrongArity errors.

3. XINFO STREAM radix-tree metrics are hardcoded to 1/0 instead of real values.

## Persistence Issues

4. DUMP uses CRC16 (2 bytes) instead of Redis's CRC64 (8 bytes). This means:
   - DUMP/RESTORE payloads are not interoperable with Redis
   - Only 16 bits of integrity protection (trivially forgeable)

5. DUMP/RESTORE bounds checks for non-String types use payload.len() instead of payload.len()-11, allowing the parser to read into the trailer bytes.

## Previously Fixed (for reference)

- XCLAIM/XAUTOCLAIM idle time <= vs < (fixed)
- Stream last-ID watermark (fixed)
- XREADGROUP pending replay delivery count (fixed)
- Stream RDB persistence (fixed — streams now saved/loaded)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:10.066810608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.516049261Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.515529597Z","close_reason":"Epic acknowledged — children tasks now unblocked for implementation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

The local client_cmd branches at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:11248-11365 currently validate their arguments and return legacy/runtime responses without any script_nesting_level guard. Add Redis-compatible rejection after the existing legacy-address/filter, timeout/mode, and arity validation paths, with Lua coverage for KILL, PAUSE WRITE/ALL, and UNPAUSE.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:03:38.448712359Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:27:46.986027586Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:27:46.983613926Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-bxlo","title":"Build redis-benchmark-compatible TCP load generator with HdrHistogram latency recording","description":"# Benchmark Load Generator

## What
A Rust binary that connects to a running FrankenRedis (or Redis) instance via TCP, sends configurable workloads, and records precise latency histograms.

## Why
The project claims "profile-proven performance" but has zero throughput or latency measurements. This tool is the foundation for ALL performance claims.

## Implementation
1. New binary: `crates/fr-server/src/bin/fr_benchmark.rs` (or dedicated crate `crates/fr-bench/`)
2. Use `std::net::TcpStream` (blocking OK for benchmark client)
3. Workloads to implement:
   - SET: random keys, configurable value size (default 3 bytes like redis-benchmark)
   - GET: random existing keys
   - INCR: atomic counter
   - LPUSH/LPOP: list operations
   - HSET/HGET: hash operations
   - Mixed: configurable ratio of reads/writes
4. Parameters: `--host`, `--port`, `--clients` (concurrent connections), `--requests` (total), `--pipeline` (commands per batch), `--keyspace` (number of unique keys), `--datasize`
5. Latency recording: use `hdrhistogram` crate for O(1) percentile queries
6. Output: JSON with ops/sec, p50/p95/p99/p999 latency in microseconds, total time, bytes transferred
7. Human-readable summary to stdout

## Validation
- Run against both frankenredis and legacy redis on same machine
- Verify ops/sec is within 2x of legacy redis for basic SET/GET
- Verify latency histogram is populated and percentiles are sane

## Files
- crates/fr-bench/Cargo.toml (new crate)
- crates/fr-bench/src/main.rs
- Cargo.toml workspace members update","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:10:23.067263275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:27:03.242987153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:27:03.242608697Z","close_reason":"Implemented fr-bench TCP benchmark harness with histogram reporting and remote-verified validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-tvv","title":"Wire CONFIG SET client-query-buffer-limit and proto-max-bulk-len to fr-server","description":"## Problem

fr-server/src/main.rs line 36 defines `const QUERY_BUFFER_LIMIT: usize = 1024 * 1024` (1 MiB). Redis defaults are:
- client-query-buffer-limit: 1 GiB
- proto-max-bulk-len: 512 MiB

The current 1 MiB limit is 1000x too restrictive. Any client sending a bulk string > 1 MiB (e.g., a large SET value) gets disconnected. This breaks real-world Redis usage patterns.

Additionally, fr-config/src/lib.rs line 66-67 has `CompatibilityGate` defaults of max_bulk_len=8MiB which also contradicts the config table's proto-max-bulk-len=512000000.

## What Redis Does

Both parameters are configurable via CONFIG SET. The query buffer limit controls the maximum size of the client input buffer (protecting against memory abuse). The proto-max-bulk-len controls the maximum size of a single bulk string in the RESP protocol.

## Implementation Plan

1. Add `query_buffer_limit` and `proto_max_bulk_len` to ServerState
2. Handle CONFIG SET for both parameters
3. In fr-server, read the limit from runtime instead of the const
4. Ensure fr-protocol's parse_frame respects the bulk length limit
5. Update CompatibilityGate defaults to match Redis (512 MiB for proto-max-bulk-len)

## Impact

This is critical for real-world usage — 1 MiB query buffer silently breaks any application storing values > 1 MiB, which is extremely common (images, serialized objects, large JSON documents).

## Files to Modify

- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`: Add fields, CONFIG SET handler
- `crates/fr-server/src/main.rs`: Replace const with runtime value
- `crates/fr-config/src/lib.rs`: Fix CompatibilityGate defaults","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:47.750082912Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:52:35.152525896Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:52:35.151919107Z","close_reason":"query_buffer_limit (1 GiB default) and proto_max_bulk_len (512 MiB default) now on ServerState, CONFIG SET/GET wired, fr-server reads runtime values","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-tvv","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-kgn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:51.850699530Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-txo","title":"Fix RESP parser read_line off-by-one at MAX_LINE_LENGTH boundary","description":"## Problem

fr-protocol/src/lib.rs line 260: The read_line loop condition \"while i + 1 < max_search\" means the last position checked for \\r is max_search-2. If \\r falls at exactly max_search-1 (offset start + MAX_LINE_LENGTH - 1) with \\n at max_search, the valid \\r\\n pair is missed and the line is incorrectly rejected as LineTooLong.

## Impact

Low — this only affects RESP lines whose content (excluding \\r\\n) is exactly MAX_LINE_LENGTH-1 bytes long (64 KiB - 1). This is a rare edge case but violates the principle that any valid RESP frame should parse correctly.

## Fix

Change the loop condition from \"i + 1 < max_search\" to \"i + 1 <= max_search\" (or equivalently, \"i < max_search\"). Ensure the i+1 bounds check for \\n doesn't read past input.len().

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs: read_line loop condition","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:03.775225391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:51.062354543Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:34:51.061004207Z","close_reason":"Fixed read_line loop to check full MAX_LINE_LENGTH range before declaring LineTooLong","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
